message:

@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cert-manager-294-how-do-i-force-a-cert-renewal-after-adding-new-entries-to-dnsna",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cert-manager: How do I force a cert renewal after adding new entries to dnsNames?",
+    "description": "How do I force a cert renewal after adding new entries to dnsNames?. This issue affects 19+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify cert-manager troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your cert-manager deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review cert-manager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager\n```\nI added a new entry in `Certificate`  `dnsNames` list\nbut after applying the new yaml, the status is still stuck in `Certificate scheduled for renewal in x hours`\n\nIs there anyway I can force a renewal of current certificate?"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for How do I force a cert renewal after adding new entries to…",
+        "description": "Ensures it's possible to update dnsNames on a certificate in order to trigger a re-issuer of a certificate.\n```yaml\nI tried using cert-manager command to refresh the cert, but I get:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm How do I force a cert renewal after adding new… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Ensures it's possible to update dnsNames on a certificate in order to trigger a re-issuer of a certificate.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "I tried using cert-manager command to refresh the cert, but I get:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
